body:
## Goal
Provide a minimal, dependency-free Node.js HTTP server that serves a Hello World page so the project has a runnable entry point.

## Spec
Use the Node.js built-in `http` module only — no external web framework or other runtime dependencies. Source is ESM (package.json `"type": "module"`). The server listens on `process.env.PORT`, defaulting to `3000` when unset. A `GET /` request returns HTTP status 200, header `Content-Type: text/html`, and a body containing the exact text `Hello, World!`. The npm `start` script launches the server via `node <source-file>`. Implementation is confined to a single source file plus package.json. Request routing/method handling beyond `GET /` is left to the deliverer's discretion (a sensible default is 404 for other paths).

## Changes
- `server.js` — creates
- `package.json` — refactors-existing

## Acceptance
- [ ] AC-1: package.json declares "type": "module" and a "start" script that runs the single source file with node, with no runtime dependencies listed
- [ ] AC-2: Starting the server with PORT unset listens on port 3000; when PORT is set the server listens on that port
- [ ] AC-3: A GET request to "/" returns HTTP 200 with a Content-Type header of text/html and a response body containing the exact text "Hello, World!"
- [ ] AC-4: The implementation consists of exactly one source file plus package.json and imports no external web framework

## Verify
- PORT=3100 node server.js & pid=$!; sleep 1; curl -si http://localhost:3100/ | tee /tmp/resp.txt; kill $pid; grep -q '^HTTP/1.1 200' /tmp/resp.txt && grep -qi '^Content-Type: text/html' /tmp/resp.txt && grep -q 'Hello, World!' /tmp/resp.txt (e2e)
- node --check server.js (validate)

## Non-Goals
- No external web framework (Express, Fastify, etc.) or other runtime dependencies
- No HTTPS/TLS, logging framework, or configuration system beyond the PORT env var
- No additional routes or static-asset serving beyond GET /

<!-- meta: {"reason_to_exist":"The project needs a minimal, runnable HTTP entry point that returns a Hello World response."} -->

title: Minimal Node.js ESM Hello World HTTP server
